SEARCH
Package org.javalabs.jpa.descriptor

Class EntityClassImpl

java.lang.Object
org.javalabs.jpa.descriptor.EntityClassImpl
All Implemented Interfaces:
ClassDescriptor
public class EntityClassImpl extends Object implements ClassDescriptor
Concrete class to represent a managed class/jpa entity.

A jpa entity is identified by the Entity annotation. Object of this class will hold the entity metadata details. If an entity has a super class identified by MappedSuperclass, then the metadata of all those classes will be kept here.

Author:
Sudiptasish Chanda

  • Constructor Details

    • EntityClassImpl

      EntityClassImpl(Class<?> clazz)

  • Method Details

    • init

      private void init(Class<?> clazz)
      Configure the entity mapping.
      Parameters:
      clazz -

    • processPk

      private void processPk()
      Process the primary key attribute(s). Although the primary key attributes are stored along with regular column metadata, but a separate store is maintained to store the method mapping for a primary key object.

    • processNamedQueries

      private void processNamedQueries(Class<?> clazz)
      If the entity class has named or native query defined, then extract them as part of screening process and store them in NamedQueryStore.
      Parameters:
      clazz - Entity class

    • processAttribute

      private void processAttribute(Class<?> clazz, Field field, EntityAttribute attribute)
      Process a regular attribute from the entity.
      Parameters:
      field -

    • entityClass

      public Class<?> entityClass()
      Description copied from interface: ClassDescriptor
      Return the java type of the entity/managed class. The identification criteria of an entity is Entity annotation. If no such annotation is present, then the framework will fail to initialize.
      Specified by:
      entityClass in interface ClassDescriptor
      Returns:
      Class

    • table

      public String table()
      Description copied from interface: ClassDescriptor
      Return the table name that is represented by this entity class. The table definition is obtained from Table annotation.
      Specified by:
      table in interface ClassDescriptor
      Returns:
      String

    • attributeCount

      public int attributeCount()
      Description copied from interface: ClassDescriptor
      Return the number of attributes this entity has.
      Specified by:
      attributeCount in interface ClassDescriptor
      Returns:
      int

    • primaryKey

      public Class<?> primaryKey()
      Description copied from interface: ClassDescriptor
      Return the java type of the primary key. Because it is advisable all tables to have a primary key, therefore, it is mandatory for all entity/managed class to have a primary key attribute. It can be a composite key or a single primary key. In either case the primary key class must be associated with the entity class via IdClass annotation.
      Specified by:
      primaryKey in interface ClassDescriptor
      Returns:
      Class

    • idCount

      public int idCount()
      Description copied from interface: ClassDescriptor
      Return the column count representing the primary key.
      Specified by:
      idCount in interface ClassDescriptor
      Returns:
      int
